item.title: Russian Oil Price at Highest Since 2023 Boosts Kremlin Windfall
item.description: Russia is getting a further boost from the Iran war as the price for its flagship Urals oil export blend reached the highest since October 2023 for tax purposes this month.

item.title: JPMorgan Strategist Says Winners in Global Stocks Go Beyond AI
item.description: Global stock markets are offering returns that in some cases are beating the S&P 500 Index, with technology companies comprising a small part of some strategies, according to Paul Quinsee at JPMorgan Asset Management.

item.title: Fermi Reworks Board Rules to Thwart Ex-CEO’s Push to Remake Firm
item.description: Texas data-center developer Fermi Inc. changed its rules around director appointments to block efforts by its former chief executive officer to reshape the company’s board.

item.title: Mobile Carriers Join Forces to Boost Coverage in Dead Zones
item.description: AT&T Inc., T-Mobile US Inc. and Verizon Communications Inc. announced a rare joint venture Thursday that aims to make satellite capabilities more widely available to mobile phone customers.

item.title: Africa E-Commerce Giant Jumia to Cut Workforce Due to AI
item.description: Jumia Technologies AG is planning to cut an initial 10% of its workforce of about 2,000 people as the African e-commerce giant implements artificial intelligence across its departments, Chief Executive Officer Francis Dufay said.

item.title: Motorola’s Razr Fold Is a Solid Phone Arriving at the Worst Time
item.description: The company’s first book-style foldable has a lot to recommend it, but a wave of fresh competition is coming soon.

item.title: Newsom’s California Budget Bolstered by Extra Cash from AI Boom
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom unveiled a revised budget that shows no deficit for this year and next, as the state draws another boost from the technology and artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: Soaring Household Defaults Rock Argentina’s Banks and Fintechs
item.description: A sharp rise in household delinquencies is shaking Argentina’s banks and fintech firms, presenting another challenge to President Javier Milei’s ambitious economic overhaul.

item.title: US Retail Sales Moderate as Gas Surge Crimps Budgets
item.description: US retail sales rose at a slower pace in April, suggesting high gasoline prices prompted consumers to temper their spending at some merchants.

item.title: US Stock Futures Gain as AI Trade Drives Tech Rally, Cisco Soars
item.description: US stock futures rose Thursday morning as the artificial intelligence trade continued to drive gains across technology shares, powered by upbeat earnings from Cisco Systems Inc. and an AI IPO.

item.title: Tate & Lyle Gets Up to £2.7 Billion Offer From Ingredion
item.description: Tate & Lyle Plc has received a takeover offer from Ingredion Inc., raising the prospect of another famous brand leaving London’s stock market.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 14, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York. Equities look set to extend Wednesday’s records with tech bulls egged on by Cisco’s premarket surge and the huge appetite shown for an AI chip IPO.

item.title: New Fortress Energy Takes $6.5 Billion Debt Plan to London Court
item.description: Billionaire Wes Edens’ New Fortress Energy Inc. is seeking permission from a UK court for a sweeping restructuring that will see the vast majority of the firm’s debt load converted into equity and hand ownership of the business to creditors.

item.title: Global Fund Managers Playing Catch-Up May Fuel S&P’s Next Leg Up
item.description: While US stocks have been going from one record to the next, global asset allocators haven’t quite kept up. And that may keep the market’s upward momentum intact as fund managers try to catch up.

item.title: Europe Is Turning Off Solar Just as Gas Market Tightens
item.description: Vast amounts of electricity are going to waste because grids can’t cope.

item.title: Brookfield Corp. Posts Profit as Firm Continues Insurance Push
item.description: Brookfield Corp. is merging its shares with those of its insurance business as the firm pushes ahead with its plans to transform itself into an investment-led insurer, the company said in its first-quarter earnings statement.

item.title: Amazon’s AI Success Sends Stock Racing Toward $3 Trillion Club
item.description: Investors are growing increasingly optimistic about Amazon.com Inc.’s position in artificial intelligence, lighting a fire under the stock and sending the company’s market capitalization soaring toward the rarefied $3 trillion level.

item.title: Trump-Xi Summit Seeks to Cement Stability After Rocky Stretch for Trade Ties
item.description: Day one featured a few highlights, including China’s tough stance on Taiwan

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise as Alibaba, TSMC Join AI Hot Streak
item.description: Emerging-market stocks advanced for the third day this week as technology companies in Taiwan and China joined a hot streak by South Korean peers, buoyed by optimism that talks between the US and China will improve trade ties and boost demand for artificial intelligence-related hardware and services.

item.title: Powell McCormick Is Fast Becoming Meta’s Face on the Global Stage
item.description: The social media company’s vice chair is part of the group traveling with Trump to China

item.title: Revolut Eyes Private Bank Arm for UK, Europe to Push Into Wealth
item.description: Revolut is looking to open up a private bank in UK and some European countries in an effort to reel in revenue from wealthy individuals, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: UK Economy Looks to Have Peaked for 2026 Amid War Headwinds
item.description: Solid GDP growth last quarter isn’t expected to last, according to Bloomberg Economics

item.title: DP World Still ‘Bullish’ on Africa Even as War Stokes Inflation
item.description: DP World Ltd. remains “bullish” on its expansion plans in Africa, even as the continent faces inflation from the Iran war caused by surging fuel prices, according to its regional head.

item.title: The Woman Selling Longevity to Women Left Out of the Boys Club
item.description: Influencer Kayla Barnes-Lentz has built a loyal following arguing that wellness protocols based on male-dominated science don’t cut it for half the population.

item.title: OPEC+ Has Plan to Complete Series of Quota Hikes, Delegates Say
item.description: Key OPEC+ members aim to continue a series of oil quota increases over the next few months, completing the return of a layer of halted production — if only on paper — by the end of September, delegates said.

item.title: The Debt Binge That Has Alphabet Execs Working the Night Shift
item.description: Google is hawking bonds in dollars, euros, pounds, francs and yen.

item.title: DayOne Is Said to Consider Upsizing Funding Round to $4 Billion
item.description: DayOne Data Centers Ltd. is considering upsizing its Series C funding round to more than $4 billion, according to people familiar with the situation, amid strong investor interest in assets supporting an artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Czech Defense Billionaire Comes Out Fighting After Share Drop
item.description: Czech billionaire Michal Strnad said investors are undervaluing his company, CSG NV, after its share price halved since he completed the largest-ever initial public offering for a pure-play defense firm.

item.title: Singapore Air Net Profit Drops as Iran War Raises Risks
item.description: Singapore Airlines Ltd. posted a steep drop in annual net profit as widening losses at Air India weighed on results, and flagged a cautious outlook that factors in risks from the Iran war.

item.title: India’s Factory-Gate Inflation Jumps on Higher Energy Prices
item.description: India’s producer prices surged to just over a three-and-a-half-year high in April as elevated energy prices pushed up manufacturers’ input costs.
